$comment: Provider-agnostic ACTIVATION FUNNEL dashboard spec for Project 50. Illustrative config — NOT a native export of any one tool. On import, map `query` to your warehouse SQL / Amplitude / PostHog and adapt the `events` table & `props` JSON access to your schema. Grounded in apps/web/lib/analytics.ts and docs/ANALYTICS.md §2.
title: Project 50 — Activation funnel
description: Visit -> signup -> project50_started -> first perfect day (7/7) -> day-50 completion.
target: warehouse (SQL); adapt for Amplitude/PostHog funnel builder on import

source.notes: Events emitted by track() in apps/web/lib/analytics.ts. `props` is JSON. `signup` is reserved (no call site yet); `day50_completed` is a recommended addition — until both exist, derive those steps from the product DB.

panels.title: Funnel — counts by step
panels.metric: Distinct users reaching each step (signup -> started -> perfect day)
panels.query: WITH signups AS (SELECT user_id, MIN(ts) AS signed_up_at FROM events WHERE event = 'signup' AND ts >= NOW() - INTERVAL '${window_days} days' GROUP BY user_id), started AS (SELECT DISTINCT user_id FROM events WHERE event = 'project50_started' AND (props->>'restarted') = 'false'), perfect_day AS (SELECT user_id FROM events WHERE event = 'rule_toggled' AND (props->>'done') = 'true' GROUP BY user_id, DATE(ts) HAVING COUNT(DISTINCT props->>'ruleId') >= 7) SELECT COUNT(*) AS signups, COUNT(*) FILTER (WHERE s.user_id IN (SELECT user_id FROM started)) AS started, COUNT(*) FILTER (WHERE s.user_id IN (SELECT user_id FROM perfect_day)) AS perfect_day FROM signups s;

panels.title: Activation rate (started / signup)
panels.metric: Share of signups that started their first challenge
panels.query: SELECT COUNT(DISTINCT st.user_id)::float / NULLIF(COUNT(DISTINCT su.user_id), 0) AS activation_rate FROM (SELECT DISTINCT user_id FROM events WHERE event = 'signup') su LEFT JOIN (SELECT DISTINCT user_id FROM events WHERE event = 'project50_started' AND (props->>'restarted') = 'false') st USING (user_id);

panels.title: Challenges started per day
panels.metric: Daily count of project50_started (first starts vs restarts)
panels.query: SELECT DATE(ts) AS day, COUNT(*) FILTER (WHERE (props->>'restarted') = 'false') AS first_starts, COUNT(*) FILTER (WHERE (props->>'restarted') = 'true') AS restarts FROM events WHERE event = 'project50_started' AND ts >= NOW() - INTERVAL '${window_days} days' GROUP BY day ORDER BY day;

panels.title: Reached a perfect day (7/7)
panels.metric: Share of starters who hit at least one all-7-rules day — the strongest early predictor of finishing
panels.query: WITH started AS (SELECT DISTINCT user_id FROM events WHERE event = 'project50_started' AND (props->>'restarted') = 'false'), perfect AS (SELECT user_id FROM events WHERE event = 'rule_toggled' AND (props->>'done') = 'true' GROUP BY user_id, DATE(ts) HAVING COUNT(DISTINCT props->>'ruleId') >= 7) SELECT COUNT(DISTINCT p.user_id)::float / NULLIF(COUNT(DISTINCT s.user_id), 0) AS perfect_day_rate FROM started s LEFT JOIN perfect p USING (user_id);

panels.title: Upgrade intent (upgrade_clicked)
panels.metric: Daily upgrade_clicked, split by whether a trial was requested
panels.query: SELECT DATE(ts) AS day, COUNT(*) FILTER (WHERE (props->>'trial') = 'true') AS with_trial, COUNT(*) FILTER (WHERE (props->>'trial') = 'false') AS without_trial FROM events WHERE event = 'upgrade_clicked' AND ts >= NOW() - INTERVAL '${window_days} days' GROUP BY day ORDER BY day;
